opp.name: AES_2025
opp.next_step: 6/1/26 MQ Bradford said the RFP is still viable but has undergone some changes that have required additional authorizations and signatures. Should be released by EOM. 4/29/26 MQ Reached out to Bradford for update on RFP. It's still not available on Ariba Portal 4/2/26 MQ RFP is still viable and is going through finalization process. It should be ready to be released by end of April. 2/12/26 MQ. Response from Bradford regarding RFP release: Good Afternoon Marc - Thanks for reaching out. The RFP is still happening. We have just been experiencing significant delays internally to get the document released. Largely due to my role change (it's going well!). I sincerely apologize about the delay. I will be scheduling a meeting with our internal team next week to get the greenlight on the content. I hope to have an update for you next week on the schedule. Once I release the RFP in ARIBA, all of my responsibilities will transition over to Syquan. Thank you again for your patience. Please reach out if you have any more questions. 12/26/25 MQ Due to Holidays and PTO RFP release has been pushed to January. 11/26/25 MQ Received updated RFP release date/timeline from Bradford. Bradford is moving to a new role at AES and will be transitioning his duties to Syquan Perrett. Milestone Estimated Date RFP Release Date 12/10/2025 Questions Submission Deadline 12/23/2025 AES response to Participant Questions 1/7/2026 Participant Proposal Submission Deadline 1/16/2026
opp.ais_why:
• The July 7 2025 Avoma call with Neha Suryawanshi and Bradford Simpson centered on RFI logistics, category management, spend analysis and supplier risk. Zycus presented the Merlin AI suite including AutoClass, conversational Merlin Assist via Teams, Autonomous Quick Source, AP Smartdesk fraud checks and contract clause-level risk scoring. The buyer responses confirmed alignment without explicit AI demand.
• Action items reduced to Zycus sending the presentation to AES and Neha issuing the RFI by end of month. No buyer-side verbatim on AI, Merlin or Agentic was captured. The conversation kept returning to SAP S4HANA P2P integration, level-4 category granularity and supplier risk heat maps rather than autonomous negotiation or agentic workflows.
• Full transcript reviewed via keyword filter confirms the direction. One substantive call provides a clean evidence base, but the RFP has slipped twice from December 2025 to April 2026 due to internal personnel changes, suggesting a weak compelling event. AI importance is rated Important on paper but no champion-level buyer-stated AI demand sits behind it.

items.quote: I know our managers would be involved in approving this project.

items.area: Direct relationship with new process owner Syquan Perrett
items.quote: Once I release the RFP in ARIBA, all of my responsibilities will transition over to Syquan.

items.area: Formal RFP evaluation criteria and timeline
items.quote: We will be issuing the RFS by end of this month.

metrics.narrative: On the 07 Jul 2025 discovery call Neha Suryawanshi articulated clear qualitative pain: 'what you're looking for when it comes to category management and spend analytics is, of course, like, spend analysis, what spend we have. Because right now, we don't have anything for sure,' plus a need for level-4 category granularity and analytic visualization by supplier and material group. She confirmed AES Clean Energy has no spend analytics and no supplier risk tool today. However, no quantified impact was ever stated - no savings target, cycle-time goal, headcount efficiency, or ROI number - and Bradford confirmed they are 'still building the business case.' Quantifying the value case with AES finance during or ahead of the RFP is the open move.

champion.narrative: Champion strength is weak and at risk because of an incomplete handoff. Bradford Simpson was the working champion from Jul 2025 through early 2026 - engaged on the discovery call and responsive to outreach - but he changed roles and explicitly transferred ownership to Syquan Perrett (Project Management Officer). Zycus has not yet built a direct relationship with Syquan: outreach was emailed to him on 02 Apr 2026 with no recorded reply, and there is no call or handoff meeting on record. Bradford is now a passive informant (last reply 01 Jun 2026), and the 19 Jun 2026 'has the RFP been cancelled?' email drew no response. Securing Syquan as an active champion is the single highest-priority relationship move.

competition.narrative: This is a greenfield eProcurement selection for AES Clean Energy, which today runs P2P on SAP S/4HANA (SAP GUI/Fiori) with no dedicated spend or supplier-risk tool - so the strongest current competitor is inertia and the status quo, evidenced by the RFP stalling for 6+ months on internal authorizations. Salesforce lists SAP Ariba and Coupa as competitors: Ariba is the corporate sourcing portal AES uses to host the RFP (institutional familiarity), but Neha explicitly said 'within AES Clean Energy, I know that we are not currently using Ariba,' so it is not an installed incumbent at the buying unit; Coupa is field-listed only with no buyer mention on any call. Neither competitor has buyer-stated preference. Zycus's wedge is the AI-native single-platform story (Merlin embedded across modules, Autoclass for the buyer's stated data-classification pain), which Neha probed positively.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is specific and confirmed by the buyer: AES Clean Energy has no spend-analysis tool ('right now, we don't have anything for sure'), no systematic supplier risk management tool, unclassified spend data that cannot be analyzed cleanly, and a need for granular (level-4) category visibility - all against a SAP GUI/Fiori and S/4HANA backbone used for P2P. Ed Delugas mapped these to AES's public drivers (cost control, rising energy-project expenses, procurement compliance, supply-chain resilience) and Neha confirmed them. The pain owner is the Clean Energy supply-chain governance team (Neha), with buyers Bradford and now Syquan executing. The pain is real but not yet quantified.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible yet - the deal has not reached commercials, and MSA/SOW, legal review, and InfoSec have not been discussed. The only procurement-process fact known is that AES issues and manages the RFP through its Ariba sourcing portal, and that internal 'authorizations and signatures' are required to release the RFP (NextStep 01 Jun 2026). Paper-process visibility will only open once the RFP is released and Zycus is formally shortlisted; asking Syquan for the post-award procurement and legal path in writing would create early visibility.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in Salesforce contact roles or in any call. On the 07 Jul 2025 call Neha said only 'I know our managers would be involved in approving this project' and that they were 'still building the business case' with budget to be assessed later, so budget authority is diffuse and unconfirmed. No CPO, CFO, or VP Procurement has appeared on any call or activity. Identifying and reaching the budget owner (likely via Syquan Perrett as the new process owner) is a required near-term move before the RFP frames the commercial evaluation.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal RFI-then-RFP sourcing process for AES Clean Energy, co-led originally by Neha Suryawanshi (governance) and Bradford Simpson (buyer). The process map has shifted: Bradford took a new role and formally handed ownership to Syquan Perrett (Project Management Officer), per NextStep 26 Nov 2025 and 12 Feb 2026 ('Once I release the RFP in ARIBA, all of my responsibilities will transition over to Syquan'). The RFP release itself has required 'additional authorizations and signatures' internally, indicating an approval chain above the buying team that has repeatedly gated the document. The approval structure above Syquan and the post-RFP evaluation timeline are not yet visible.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (RFP hosted in Ariba portal)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation will run through a formal RFI/RFP that AES hosts on its Ariba sourcing portal, so criteria will be documented there once released. From the discovery call the buyer's stated priorities are: spend analysis and classification (level-4 category granularity, visualization by supplier and material group), supplier risk management (currently absent), category management, and integration with the existing SAP S/4HANA P2P environment. Neha probed the AI angle specifically, asking how Autoclass trains ('will it be like training data for one year, two year?'), signalling data-classification quality as a criterion. The formal weighted criteria remain unconfirmed until the RFP document is released.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score 5.0 / Moderate. The single call shows curiosity, not demand: the buyer probed how Autoclass is trained but framed the project around spend visibility, category granularity, and SAP integration rather than autonomous or agentic workflows. AI is 'Important on paper' without a champion-level AI ask behind it.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: AES Corporation — Formal Evaluation · $500K · closes 30 Sep

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 27 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 12 is anchored on the stage win (27), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

deal_movement.summary: The deal has moved backward on timing, not forward on evaluation. Amount was raised 100k to 500k (10 Dec 2025) and the close date was pushed twice (25 Mar 2026 to 29 May 2026 to 30 Sep 2026), while the RFP release slipped from 10 Dec 2025 through Jan, Apr, and Jun 2026 and remains unreleased. Ownership handed from Bradford Simpson to Syquan Perrett. All contact since Jul 2025 is rep-initiated email chasing the RFP.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: the only live buyer call is 12 months old, all contact since is rep-initiated email chasing the RFP, and the most recent chase (19 Jun 2026) went unanswered.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 08 Jul 2025 (~360 days old). Stage Formal Evaluation (pre-RFP-release) for ~360 days with no progression to RFP issuance. Close date 30 Sep 2026 (~89 days out). Last Salesforce activity 01 Jul 2026 (rep call, wrong number). Last substantive buyer reply 01 Jun 2026 (Bradford: RFP 'should be released by EOM' - end Jun 2026 passed, still unreleased). Original plan: RFP release 10 Dec 2025, proposal deadline 16 Jan 2026 - none occurred. Forward pace: RFP release, question rounds, proposal, evaluation, shortlist, selection, negotiation and contracting cannot complete by 30 Sep 2026 if the RFP has not even released; a 3-6 month slip is required.
evidence: NextStep 01 Jun 2026: Bradford Simpson stated the RFP 'is still viable but has undergone some changes that have required additional authorizations and signatures. Should be released by EOM.'
NextStep 29 Apr 2026: rep reached out to Bradford for an RFP update; it was 'still not available on Ariba Portal.'
NextStep 02 Apr 2026: RFP 'still viable and is going through finalization process. It should be ready to be released by end of April' - April passed without release.
NextStep 12 Feb 2026: Bradford: 'The RFP is still happening. We have just been experiencing significant delays internally to get the document released. Largely due to my role change.'
NextStep 26 Nov 2025: original RFP timeline (release 10 Dec 2025, proposal deadline 16 Jan 2026) - none of these milestones occurred.
Task 19 Jun 2026: rep emailed Bradford asking 'Has the RFP been cancelled?' with no recorded reply, indicating thinning buyer engagement.
Only one live buyer call on record (07 Jul 2025 introductory call); all subsequent contact is rep-initiated email with status-only replies, not live evaluation activity.
north_star_verdict.headline: Greenfield eProcurement RFP has slipped 6+ months past its December 2025 target and is still unreleased; the deal is stalled at the buyer's internal authorization layer with only rep-initiated email contact since the July 2025 discovery call.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (close date should slip to Q1 2027 until the RFP releases)

flags: Complete the champion handoff: build a direct relationship with Syquan Perrett, who now owns the RFP, before the deal stalls further.
Manufacture a compelling event: quantify the cost of no spend visibility and manual supplier-risk handling to help AES unblock the internal authorizations delaying the RFP.
Pre-frame the AI wedge: put Merlin and Autoclass in front of the buyer against the level-4 classification pain so the RFP criteria favor Zycus over Ariba and Coupa.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is stalled at the buyer's internal authorization layer with a broken champion handoff and no quantified business case; the win levers are re-anchoring on the new process owner, forcing a compelling event, and pre-framing the AI differentiation before the RFP locks criteria.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is buyer-preferred; the single strongest current threat is a no-decision / status-quo stall, evidenced by the RFP slipping 6+ months on internal AES authorizations (NextStep through 01 Jun 2026). Among named vendors, SAP Ariba ranks next only because AES hosts the RFP on its Ariba sourcing portal (institutional familiarity), though Neha confirmed 07 Jul 2025 that Clean Energy does not actually use Ariba; Coupa is a Salesforce-listed name with zero buyer mention and is the lowest active threat. The formal RFP will likely widen the shortlist once released.

competitors.name: No decision / status quo (SAP S/4HANA)
competitors.quote: The RFP is still viable but has undergone some changes that have required additional authorizations and signatures.
competitors.how_we_win: Create a compelling event: quantify the cost of no spend visibility and manual supplier-risk handling with AES finance, and give Syquan a business-case one-pager to unlock the internal authorizations.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Within AES Clean Energy, I know that we are not currently using Ariba, and we do not have a tool for supplier risk management.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 07 Jul 2025 (Neha Suryawanshi); SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's single-platform, AI-native architecture and Autoclass data classification against Ariba's bolt-on modules; note Clean Energy has no Ariba install, so there is no switching lock-in to overcome.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: No buyer mention on any call; listed in Salesforce Competitors__c only.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with 20 years of organic AI (Merlin, 32 patents) and phased adoption (start with spend analysis and sourcing, add contracts and P2P later) versus a full-suite push.

items.deliverable: Release the eProcurement RFP on the AES Ariba portal so Zycus can respond

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope maps well to the buyer's stated needs (spend analysis, category management, supplier risk, SAP integration), but the AI/agentic value has not been buyer-validated and no quantified success criteria exist yet.

• The July 7 2025 Avoma call with Neha Suryawanshi and Bradford Simpson centered on RFI logistics, category management, spend analysis and supplier risk. Zycus presented the Merlin AI suite including AutoClass, conversational Merlin Assist via Teams, Autonomous Quick Source, AP Smartdesk fraud checks and contract clause-level risk scoring. The buyer responses confirmed alignment without explicit AI demand.
• Action items reduced to Zycus sending the presentation to AES and Neha issuing the RFI by end of month. No buyer-side verbatim on AI, Merlin or Agentic was captured. The conversation kept returning to SAP S4HANA P2P integration, level-4 category granularity and supplier risk heat maps rather than autonomous negotiation or agentic workflows.
• Full transcript reviewed via keyword filter confirms the direction. One substantive call provides a clean evidence base, but the RFP has slipped twice from December 2025 to April 2026 due to internal personnel changes, suggesting a weak compelling event. AI importance is rated Important on paper but no champion-level buyer-stated AI demand sits behind it.
hard.next_step:
6/1/26 MQ Bradford said the RFP is still viable but has undergone some changes that have required additional authorizations and signatures. Should be released by EOM.


4/29/26 MQ Reached out to Bradford for update on RFP. It's still not available on Ariba Portal


4/2/26 MQ RFP is still viable and is going through finalization process. It should be ready to be released by end of April.


2/12/26 MQ. Response from Bradford regarding RFP release:

Good Afternoon Marc - Thanks for reaching out. The RFP is still happening. We have just been experiencing significant delays internally to get the document released. Largely due to my role change (it's going well!). I sincerely apologize about the delay.


I will be scheduling a meeting with our internal team next week to get the greenlight on the content. I hope to have an update for you next week on the schedule. Once I release the RFP in ARIBA, all of my responsibilities will transition over to Syquan. 


Thank you again for your patience. Please reach out if you have any more questions. 



12/26/25 MQ Due to Holidays and PTO RFP release has been pushed to January.


11/26/25 MQ Received updated RFP release date/timeline from Bradford. Bradford is moving to a new role at AES and will be transitioning his duties to Syquan Perrett.
